ci_support_linux_64_.yaml:
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
python_min:
- '3.10'

about.description:
An API browser is an application that runs locally on your computer and allows
you to search various API docs by pressing a key combination and starting to
type (I have bound it to ⌥Space bar and can’t write code without it).

The most common ones – [Dash](https://kapeli.com/dash) (macOS) and
[Zeal](https://zealdocs.org) (Windows and Linux) – come with **many** docsets
out of the box, but they can never offer you docsets for *every* package
you’ll ever use.

This is where *doc2dash* comes in:
It takes your offline documentation and converts it into an indexed format
that API browsers can read and search (very fast!).

Currently it supports all known *intersphinx*-based documentation systems
like [Sphinx](https://www.sphinx-doc.org/),
[*pydoctor*](https://github.com/twisted/pydoctor), or
[MkDocs](https://www.mkdocs.org) (with
[*mkdocstrings*](https://mkdocstrings.github.io)).

While *doc2dash* is a Python project, the support is **not** limited to
Python-related formats.

It’s also extendable:
[**you** can write your own parser](https://doc2dash.hynek.me/en/latest/extending/)!
about.summary:
doc2dash is an extensible Documentation Set (docset) generator that helps you
to have documentation for all your favorite APIs in Dash.app-compatible API browsers.

script: doc2dash --version | ${{ "grep -iE" if unix else "findstr /c" }} "${{ version | replace('.', '\\.') }}$"

about.summary:
doc2dash is an extensible Documentation Set (docset) generator that helps you
to have documentation for all your favorite APIs in Dash.app-compatible API browsers.

script: doc2dash --version | ${{ "grep -iE" if unix else "findstr /c" }} "${{ version | replace('.', '\\.') }}$"

name: doc2dash
raw_meta_yaml:
# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/prefix-dev/recipe-format/main/schema.json
schema_version: 1

context:
  version: "3.1.0"
  python_check_max: "3.14"

package:
  name: doc2dash
  version: ${{ version }}

source:
  url: https://pypi.org/packages/source/d/doc2dash/doc2dash-${{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: 37f61c8d8f6a955d2466b9c073eb2bff44cc54352bf817afb48a3ac8aef6d594

build:
  number: 2
  noarch: python
  script:
    - ${{ PYTHON }} -m pip install . -vv --no-build-isolation --no-deps --disable-pip-version-check
  python:
    entry_points:
      - doc2dash = doc2dash.__main__:main

requirements:
  host:
    - hatch-fancy-pypi-readme
    - hatch-vcs
    - hatchling
    - pip
    - python ${{ python_min }}.*
  run:
    - attrs >=23.2
    - beautifulsoup4
    - click
    - python >=${{ python_min }}
    - rich

tests:
  - python:
      imports: doc2dash
      pip_check: true
      python_version:
        - ${{ python_min }}.*
        - ${{ python_check_max }}.*
  - files:
      source:
        - tests/
    requirements:
      run:
        - pytest-cov
        - python ${{ python_min }}.*
    script:
      - doc2dash --version | ${{ "grep -iE" if unix else "findstr /c" }} "${{ version | replace('.', '\\.') }}$"
      - doc2dash --help
      - coverage run --source=doc2dash --branch -m pytest -vv --tb=long --color=yes
      - coverage report --show-missing --skip-covered --fail-under=99

about:
  summary: |
    doc2dash is an extensible Documentation Set (docset) generator that helps you
    to have documentation for all your favorite APIs in Dash.app-compatible API browsers.
  license: MIT
  license_file: LICENSE
  homepage: https://pypi.org/project/doc2dash
  repository: https://github.com/hynek/doc2dash
  documentation: https://doc2dash.hynek.me
  description: |
    An API browser is an application that runs locally on your computer and allows
    you to search various API docs by pressing a key combination and starting to
    type (I have bound it to ⌥Space bar and can’t write code without it).

    The most common ones – [Dash](https://kapeli.com/dash) (macOS) and
    [Zeal](https://zealdocs.org) (Windows and Linux) – come with **many** docsets
    out of the box, but they can never offer you docsets for *every* package
    you’ll ever use.

    This is where *doc2dash* comes in:
    It takes your offline documentation and converts it into an indexed format
    that API browsers can read and search (very fast!).

    Currently it supports all known *intersphinx*-based documentation systems
    like [Sphinx](https://www.sphinx-doc.org/),
    [*pydoctor*](https://github.com/twisted/pydoctor), or
    [MkDocs](https://www.mkdocs.org) (with
    [*mkdocstrings*](https://mkdocstrings.github.io)).

    While *doc2dash* is a Python project, the support is **not** limited to
    Python-related formats.

    It’s also extendable:
    [**you** can write your own parser](https://doc2dash.hynek.me/en/latest/extending/)!

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - pbronez
    - hoishing
    - bollwyvl
